repo_prior_art.cite: none; doc/API.md §3 says ThmSmith/PartialID is empty, and the withdrawn angle 0 kernel was generic observed-increment strict tightening

published_prior_art.cite: YinLiuGengLuo2020 and HsiaoTianParast2025 already cover observed-data surrogate-paradox compatibility/resilience, but not finite noisy latent-class proxy panels with bounded drift

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no active in-catalogue PartialID surrogate/noisy-proxy theorem found in doc/API.md §3 or active doc/formalization entries

published_prior_art.cite: none; closest are HuiWalter1980 for the two-test algebra and FinkelsteinAdamsSariaShpitser2021/DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 for generic computable bounds

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1 / Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The related-work comparison misses closer proxy-partial-ID papers, especially GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 on bounds with proxy variables and hidden mediators, Li2026 on sharp optimal-transport proxy partial identification, and HsiaoParast2026 as the current surrogate-resilience extension.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Make Conjecture 2 the explicit field-tier kernel and demote Conjecture 1 to a supporting negative example unless it is sharpened to a fixed/common-kernel counterexample.

Add a direct §5 and §8 comparison to proxy partial-identification/bridge-function work, especially Ghassami-Shpitser-Tchetgen and Li, explaining why the common Hui-Walter kernel plus historical bridge law is not their proxy/transport bound.
Define \mathcal{B}(P^B,q) with explicit existential latent endpoint kernels, not only the conditional mean m, so reproducing the full bridge law P^B is unambiguous.

recommended_next_step: Revise the literature gap and center the proposal on the common-kernel analytic boundary in Conjecture 2 before sending it back to derivation.
